title: Le Foyer du Château
description:
Le Foyer du Château

Founded in September 2017 by Miya Yoshida, Annette Maechtel, and Kirsten Palz, /le foyer du château / is an independent project space dedicated to research into urbanism and living environments. Operating with a concept of artistic production that emphasizes communal action and interdisciplinary exchange, /le foyer du château/ welcomes contributions from all disciplines. Its programming since inception has included video screenings, artist talks, reading groups, workshops, and experimental formats.

/le foyer du château /

https://lfdc.berlin/

images.alt: View from an elevated terrace over a Berlin square at sunset with construction cranes
images.caption: Terrace of /le foyer du château /

title: Art + Feminism
description:
Art + Feminism

Art+Feminism (2016–ongoing)

Since 2016, Kirsten Palz and collaborators have hosted the annual Art+Feminism Wikipedia Edit-a-thon (Berlin), an event dedicated to improving the representation of women and their contributions to the arts on Wikipedia. The edit-a-thon takes place each year on International Women’s Day and brings together friends, colleagues, and community members for a collaborative editing session.

During the event, participants are guided through the process of becoming Wikipedia editors and are encouraged to work in pairs to complete entries by the end of the evening. Art+Feminism is a global initiative with a rhizomatic structure, aimed at enhancing the visibility of women in the arts on Wikipedia while also promoting female editorship. The campaign has seen remarkable global participation.

Save the date and join us for future Art+Feminism events!

images.alt: Art+Feminism poster with a raised fist holding a paintbrush inside a female symbol
images.caption: Poster for Art + Feminism (2016)

title: Book Fairs
description:
Book Fairs

Art book fair Miss Read (2012–ongoing)

Art book fair Friends with Books (2014–ongoing)

Kirsten Palz participated as an artist and publisher in several Art book fairs such as Miss Read (since 2012) and Friends with Books (since 2014) in Berlin. Also, her script was part of a selection of Artist scripts and Artists' books shown during Art book fairs in Europe, curated by diverse curators such as Miss Read, Friends with Books, and section .a.

Artist’s Book Fairs 2014-2025

During the last 10 years, I have participated annually in the artist book fair in Berlin. I have presented my artist books and editions at the following fairs and launches:

2025 Info on Books at Café Babette Berlin.

2025 Miss read with ‘Forest Research Platform’. A selection of new books about art & ecologies. Haus der Kulturen der Welt (HKW)

2021 Freunde ohne Bücher, HAUNT frontviews, Berlin.

2019 Friends with Books, Kirsten Palz presenting selection of Song books. Hamburger Bahnhof – Museum für Gegenwart.

2018 Friends with Books, artist collective ACTA presents selection of new editions and objects. Hamburger Bahnhof – Museum für Gegenwart.

2017 Friends with Books, Kirsten Palz presents selection of own books. Hamburger Bahnhof – Museum für Gegenwart.

2016 Friends with Books, Kirsten Palz presents selection of own books. Hamburger Bahnhof – Museum für Gegenwart.

2015 Friends with Books with Erik Steinbrecher, presenting the publication; Kirsten Palz 100 works. Publisher: rakete.co. Distribution: Motto. Hamburger Bahnhof – Museum für Gegenwart.

2014 Friends with Books with Erik Steinbrecher, presenting Kirsten Palz’s ‘Object 112’. Publisher: rakete.co. Editions. Café Moskau, Berlin.

https://friendswithbooks.org

https://missread.com/

ACTA Formats and Definitions:

ACTA operates in different formats:

ACTA ACTS

refers to a series of exhibitions, happenings and actions in institutional and public space.

ACTA SCREEN

ACTA SCREEN includes film- and video formats.

ACTA AUDIO

ACTA AUDIO produces sound-files combining spoken words, digitally generated sounds, and field recordings.

ACTA PRINT

ACTA PRINT works as an independent publisher of magazines, protocols, and other formats since 2015.

ACTA PROPOSAL

ACTA PROPOSALS are sent to institutions, organisations and private individuals.

ACTA ARCHITECTURE

ACTA ARCHITECTURE is commited to built structures. It engages with urban environments, land and sustainable solutions.

ACTA TIMES

ACTA introduced an independent notational system for time in 2015. The ACTA time notational system challenges previous systems in frequency and logic. ACTA views the parameter TimeX as central for all action and concepts. TimeX=slow light defines all calculations/processes. TimeX is a starting point for experiments.

ACTA PERCEPTION

ACTA PERCEPTION pursue to strengthen the sculpture’s performative quality. The sculpture is the protagonist and point of departure for particular models, matters and processes. ACTA PERCEPTION operates within various modes of surveillance.

ACTA LANGUAGE

ACTA LANGUAGE develops -on the premise of and as a response to machine-generated intelligence combined with human presence and action. ACTA LANGUAGE explores ‘natural’ and ‘artificial' language structures. ACTA LANGUAGE as communication tools intends to create its own singularity. ACTA LANGUAGE has errors. Attentive to communicative failures, it is neither purely logical, purely communicative or purely standardised.
